title: Swollen and Hot Testicles in Dogs: Symptoms, Causes, and Urgent Care
meta_desc: If your dog's testicles are hot and swollen, it may be a veterinary emergency. Learn about orchitis, torsion, and why you need to see a vet now.
content_html:
What is it?

When a male dog's testicles become noticeably swollen, firm, or hot to the touch, it typically indicates a serious underlying condition. The most common causes are orchitis (an infection or inflammation of the testes), epididymitis, or testicular torsion, which is a rare but life-threatening twisting of the spermatic cord. These conditions are extremely painful and can lead to permanent damage or systemic illness if the infection spreads to the bloodstream.

Is it an emergency?

Yes, this is a high-priority emergency. Heat and rapid swelling in the scrotum are signs of acute inflammation or a loss of blood flow. Because it is impossible to distinguish between a localized infection and a surgical emergency like torsion without a professional exam, you should seek veterinary care immediately. Delaying treatment can lead to tissue death (necrosis) and severe distress for your pet.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a clear, well-lit photo of the affected area to your veterinary clinic or a telehealth provider can significantly speed up the triage process. A visual allows the medical team to evaluate the degree of swelling, skin discoloration, or presence of discharge. This information helps them determine the level of urgency and ensures they are fully prepared to treat your dog the moment you arrive at the clinic.

faqs.question: Can I give my dog human pain medication for the swelling?
faqs.answer: No. Never give human medications like ibuprofen or acetaminophen to dogs, as they are toxic. Your veterinarian will provide safe, pet-specific pain relief.

faqs.question: What are other symptoms of testicular issues in dogs?
faqs.answer: Watch for lethargy, fever, loss of appetite, excessive licking of the area, or a 'hunched' walking gait due to abdominal pain.

faqs.question: Can neutered dogs have scrotal swelling?
faqs.answer: While rare, a recently neutered dog can develop a scrotal hematoma (a collection of blood). This also requires immediate veterinary attention to prevent infection.
